event: Cupid Choosing an Arrow from His Quiver
symbols: winged child — Cupid or Eros, the classical god of love
quiver of arrows — the instruments of desire and passion
wings — the swiftness and unpredictability of love
description: A black-and-white engraving depicting a winged child (putto) standing and examining a large quiver full of arrows. The child has small feathered wings on its back and curly hair. It grasps the quiver with both hands, looking down at the arrows. The background is rendered with hatched shading typical of engraving technique. The composition is simple, focusing entirely on the single figure.

_meta.title: Love chooses an arrow in the quiver, by Guercino
